provenance.source_corpus: Geological aspects of the beach restoration programme in Chedabucto Bay, Nova Scotia, 1970

nodes.name: Removal of large volumes of sediment

source_spans.text: The removal of large volumes of sediment was considered very damaging to the stability of the foreshore
The removal of large volumes of sediment was considered very damaging to the stability of the foreshore

nodes.name: Use of a bulldozer to pile up material

source_spans.text: The use of a bulldozer to pile up material for removal mixes clean and oiled material

nodes.name: Mixing of clean and oiled material

source_spans.text: The use of a bulldozer to pile up material for removal mixes clean and oiled material
The disturbance of the beach material led to the washing out of fines and the release of oil into the littoral zone

nodes.name: Release of oil into the littoral zone

source_spans.text: The disturbance of the beach material led to the washing out of fines and the release of oil into the littoral zone
This action exposed the fine, till-derived, sediment which overlay the bedrock
This action exposed the fine, till-derived, sediment which overlay the bedrock
Removal of sediment is harmful as the supply of cobble size material is very limited and erosion of the till platform and backshore may result from the loss of the protecting beach apron
Removal of sediment is harmful as the supply of cobble size material is very limited and erosion of the till platform and backshore may result from the loss of the protecting beach apron
Erosion of the till platform and backshore may result from the loss of the protecting beach apron
Erosion of the till platform and backshore may result from the loss of the protecting beach apron
Beach material was used for construction purposes and this was followed by a shoreline recession of 20 feet in 50 years
Beach material was used for construction purposes and this was followed by a shoreline recession of 20 feet in 50 years
Spillage from the blade and grinding by the tracks were the major defects of this equipment... this led to grinding and mixing to depths as great as four feet
Spillage from the blade and grinding by the tracks were the major defects of this equipment... this led to grinding and mixing to depths as great as four feet
The cleaning of selected sections of a shoreline is not effective because oil from adjacent contaminated sections, as well as from offshore, can lead to reoiling
The cleaning of selected sections of a shoreline is not effective because oil from adjacent contaminated sections, as well as from offshore, can lead to reoiling
The manual method is considered less harmful to the beach as less uncontaminated sediment is removed
The manual method is considered less harmful to the beach as less uncontaminated sediment is removed

nodes.name: Construction of groynes, jetties and other harbour facilities

source_spans.text: The construction of groynes, jetties and other harbour facilities has led, in the past, to unexpected alterations of the adjacent coast
The construction of groynes, jetties and other harbour facilities has led, in the past, to unexpected alterations of the adjacent coast
Natural processes are effective in cleaning this type of beach unless wave action is limited and/or pollution is very heavy
Natural processes are effective in cleaning this type of beach unless wave action is limited and/or pollution is very heavy
